design_philosophy.core_concept: A digital compass for the Indian traveler. Visuals should feel like a premium travel magazine come to life—tactile, spacious, and inviting.

grid_system.bento_grid: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4 auto-rows-[300px]

Card.styles: bg-white/80 backdrop-blur-md border border-white/20 shadow-sm hover:shadow-md transition-all duration-300 rounded-xl overflow-hidden

MapTooltip.styles: bg-gray-900 text-white text-xs px-2 py-1 rounded shadow-xl z-50

interactive_map.instructions: Use 'react-simple-maps' for rendering the world map. Create a 'MapChart' component that accepts a 'data' prop (mapping country codes to colors). Use 'd3-scale' for color scales if needed, but direct color mapping is preferred based on the problem statement constraints.
interactive_map.interactions: Hovering over a country should slightly darken the fill and show a tooltip with the country name and relevant info (Season/Visa). Clicking a country should navigate to a detailed view or open a modal.

visa_section.description: Map, camera, watch - Visa Section Background

instructions_to_main_agent: 2. Theme: Implement the 'Modern Atlas' theme in 'tailwind.config.js' (extend colors with map_colors).
3. Map Implementation: Create a reusable WorldMap component. It MUST take a 'mode' prop ('seasons' | 'visa') to switch coloring logic.
4. Data: Mock the season and visa data for at least 20 major countries (USA, UK, France, Dubai, Thailand, Singapore, Australia, etc.) to show the color variations.
5. Forex: Mock the FOREX API call first, then try to connect to a free API (like 'exchangerate-api.com' or similar free tier). If API fails, fallback to mock data gracefully.
6. Apps: Create a filterable grid for the Apps section. Use the 'Bento' grid layout.
7. Navigation: Use a sticky glassmorphic header.
8. Testing: Add 'data-testid' to all interactive elements (maps, buttons, filters).

do_not_use: Default browser scrollbars (use 'lenis' or custom CSS)
